text: Liver steatosis, characterized by excessive accumulation of lipids in hepatocytes, predisposes cells to injury and death due to lipotoxicity. Excessive lipid accumulation leads to oxidative stress, mitochondrial dysfunction, and endoplasmic reticulum stress. These cellular disturbances compromise membrane integrity, impair energy homeostasis, and activate apoptotic and necrotic pathways, resulting in cell injury and death. Chronic steatosis amplifies inflammatory signaling, creating a self-perpetuating cycle of liver damage (PMID: 37400694).
